english_literature_remaining_courses.title: Literary Theory and Criticism
english_literature_remaining_courses.description: Introduction to major schools of literary theory and criticism including formalism, structuralism, poststructuralism, feminism, and postcolonialism. Students learn to apply theoretical frameworks to literary analysis and understand critical discourse.
learning_objectives: Understand major schools of literary theory and criticism
Apply theoretical frameworks to literary analysis
Evaluate different critical approaches and their assumptions
Develop sophisticated analytical and interpretive skills
Engage with contemporary critical discourse

english_literature_remaining_courses.title: Digital Humanities
english_literature_remaining_courses.description: Application of digital tools and methods to literary and cultural studies including text analysis, data visualization, and digital archives. Students learn to use technology for literary research and explore new forms of scholarly communication.
learning_objectives: Apply digital tools to literary analysis and research
Understand data visualization and text mining techniques
Create digital projects and presentations
Evaluate digital archives and online resources
Explore new forms of scholarly communication
covered_materials: Copyright and intellectual property in digital contexts

english_literature_remaining_courses.title: Women's Literature
english_literature_remaining_courses.description: Study of literature by women writers across periods and cultures examining themes of gender, identity, and social experience. Students analyze works by women authors while exploring feminist literary criticism and theory.
learning_objectives: Analyze literature by women writers from various periods
Examine themes of gender, identity, and social experience
Apply feminist literary criticism and theory
Understand historical contexts of women's writing
Evaluate the recovery and canonization of women's literature
covered_materials: Women of color and multicultural perspectives
Women's literary traditions and recovery projects

english_literature_remaining_courses.title: Multicultural Literature
english_literature_remaining_courses.description: Exploration of literature by writers from diverse ethnic, racial, and cultural backgrounds including African American, Latino/a, Asian American, and Native American authors. Students examine themes of identity, diaspora, and cultural intersection.
learning_objectives: Analyze literature from diverse ethnic and cultural backgrounds
Examine themes of identity, diaspora, and cultural intersection
Understand historical and social contexts of multicultural writing
Apply postcolonial and ethnic literary criticism
Evaluate issues of representation and cultural authenticity
covered_materials: Native American literature and oral traditions

english_literature_remaining_courses.title: Genre Studies
english_literature_remaining_courses.description: Intensive study of specific literary genres such as the novel, short story, or drama, examining formal conventions, historical development, and cultural significance. Students analyze how genres evolve and influence literary expression.
learning_objectives: Understand formal conventions and characteristics of specific genres
Trace historical development and evolution of literary forms
Analyze cultural and social significance of genre conventions
Examine genre theory and classification systems
Evaluate contemporary innovations and genre blending
covered_materials: Cultural and social functions of genres

english_literature_remaining_courses.title: Literature and Media
english_literature_remaining_courses.description: Examination of relationships between literature and other media including film, television, digital media, and graphic novels. Students analyze adaptations, intermediality, and the evolution of storytelling across platforms.
learning_objectives: Analyze relationships between literature and visual media
Evaluate film and television adaptations of literary works
Understand intermediality and cross-media storytelling
Examine digital media and new forms of narrative
Assess the impact of technology on literary expression
covered_materials: Literature to film adaptation theory and practice
Graphic novels and comics as literature

english_literature_remaining_courses.title: Professional Writing
english_literature_remaining_courses.description: Development of writing skills for professional contexts including business communication, technical writing, and digital content creation. Students learn to adapt writing for various professional audiences and purposes.
learning_objectives: Develop professional writing skills for various contexts
Adapt writing style for different professional audiences
Create effective business and technical documents
Understand digital content creation and management
Apply project management principles to writing projects
covered_materials: Web content and digital marketing copy

english_literature_remaining_courses.title: Publishing and Editorial Studies
english_literature_remaining_courses.description: Introduction to the publishing industry including traditional and digital publishing, editing processes, and literary market dynamics. Students learn about the business of literature and develop editorial skills.
learning_objectives: Understand the structure and economics of the publishing industry
Develop editing and proofreading skills
Learn about digital and traditional publishing processes
Understand literary market dynamics and audience development
Explore career opportunities in publishing and media

english_literature_remaining_courses.title: Teaching Literature
english_literature_remaining_courses.description: Pedagogical approaches to teaching literature at various levels including curriculum development, assessment strategies, and classroom management. Students learn effective methods for engaging students with literary texts.
learning_objectives: Develop effective strategies for teaching literature
Create engaging curriculum and lesson plans
Apply various pedagogical approaches to literary instruction
Understand assessment and evaluation in literature education
Address diverse learning needs and cultural backgrounds

english_literature_remaining_courses.title: Advanced Creative Writing
english_literature_remaining_courses.description: Advanced workshop in creative writing focusing on developing individual voice and style in a chosen genre. Students produce substantial creative work while engaging in sophisticated critique and revision processes.
learning_objectives: Develop advanced skills in a chosen creative writing genre
Produce substantial original creative work
Engage in sophisticated workshop critique and discussion
Understand contemporary literary markets and publication
Develop individual voice and artistic vision
covered_materials: Advanced craft techniques in chosen genre
Reading as a writer - studying craft
Building a writing practice and routine

english_literature_remaining_courses.title: Cultural History
english_literature_remaining_courses.description: Study of cultural movements, intellectual trends, and social practices across historical periods. Students examine how cultural expressions reflect and shape historical change while understanding the relationship between culture and society.
learning_objectives: Understand major cultural movements and intellectual trends
Analyze relationships between culture and historical change
Examine various forms of cultural expression
Apply historical methods to cultural analysis
Connect past cultural developments to contemporary issues

english_literature_remaining_courses.title: Introduction to Linguistics
english_literature_remaining_courses.description: Introduction to the scientific study of language including phonetics, phonology, morphology, syntax, semantics, and pragmatics. Students learn fundamental concepts about language structure and use while exploring linguistic diversity.
learning_objectives: Understand fundamental concepts in linguistic analysis
Analyze language structure at various levels
Examine language variation and change
Apply linguistic methods to language data
Understand relationships between language and society
